lines.en_lit: or perhaps calculated excluding the cubit of the base and the cubit of ledge? shall stand

if both of them were equal the appointed says Extend fingers hatzbiu taught Put out your fingers for a count. Let him count themselves a support for Rabbi Yitzḥak, as Rabbi Yitzḥak said: It is prohibited to count Jews even for the purposes of a mitzva, as it is written “And he numbered them with bezek”

Rav Ashi strongly objects to this From where that this bezek is a term to break apart, Perhaps it is the name of a town, as it is written: “And they found Adoni-bezek but from here, “And Saul summoned the people and numbered them by sheep”

Rabbi Elazar said: Whoever counts Jews violates a negative as it is stated: “And the number of the children of Israel will be like the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured” Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: violates two negative as it is stated cannot be measured and cannot be counted”

Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan raised a contradiction: It is written “And the number of the children of Israel will be like the sand of the sea,” “Which cannot be measured and cannot be counted,”

not difficult: Here, a time when the Jewish people fulfill the will of God; There, a time when do not fulfill the will of God; Rabbi said in the name of Abba Yosei ben Dostai: not difficult: Here, by the hand of man; There, by the hand of God,

Rav Nehilai bar Idi said Shmuel said: Once a man is appointed as a leader of the community, he becomes wealthy. Initially, it is written “And he numbered them with bezek,” and in the end it is written: “And he numbered them with sheep,” But perhaps from their own If so, what is the novelty in the matter?

and he strove in the valley” Rabbi Mani said: concerning the matter of the valley. At the time when the Holy One, Blessed be He, said to go and attack Amalek said: Now, if one life the Torah said to bring a heifer whose neck is broken all the more so all these lives.

And If the men have sinned, what have the animals sinned? And if the adults have sinned, what have the children sinned? A Divine Voice came forth and said to him: “Do not be overly righteous” when Saul said to Doeg: “Turn around and strike down the priests, a Divine Voice came forth and said to him: “Do not be overly wicked”

Rav Huna said: How little does a person who has the support of his Lord have to worry or be concerned. Saul with one and it was counted against him, David, with two and not counted against him, What was Saul’s one The incident Agag, But There is the incident of Nob, the city of priests, the incident Agag, “I regret that I have crowned Saul to be king”
David with two What were they? Uriah the incitement
But There is also the incident of Bathsheba, There, punishment was exacted from him as it is written “And he shall restore the lamb fourfold” child Amnon Tamar, and Avshalom
There, too, he was punished as it is written: “And the Lord sent a plague against Israel from the morning until the appointed time” There, was not punished in his body;

There, too, was not punished in his body; not so; he was punished in his body, Rav Yehuda said Rav said: David was stricken with leprosy six months and the Sanhedrin withdrew from him and the Divine Presence left him. As it is written “May those who fear You return to me, and they who know Your testimonies” And it is written “Restore to me the joy of Your salvation,
Didn’t Rav say: David accepted a slanderous report Shmuel, who said: David did not accept a slanderous report,

And even to Rav, who said that David accepted a slanderous report, was he not punished As Rav Yehuda said Rav said: At the time when David said to Mephibosheth: “I say that you and Ziba should divide the field” a Divine Voice came forth and said to him: Rehoboam and Jeroboam will divide the kingship. )
“Saul was one year old when he began to reign” Rav Huna said: like a one-year–old, in that he had never tasted the taste of sin

Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak strongly objects to this say like a one-year-old filthy with mud and excrement. Rav Naḥman was shown a frightful dream He said: I humbly submit myself to you, O bones of Saul, son of Kish, once again he was shown a frightful dream, said I humbly submit myself to you, O bones of Saul, son of Kish, king of Israel,

Rav Yehuda said Shmuel said: Why did the kingship of the house of Saul not continue because there was no flaw As Rabbi Yoḥanan said in the name of Rabbi Shimon ben Yehotzadak: One appoints a leader over the community only if he has a box of creeping animals hanging behind him, so that if he exhibits a haughty attitude toward the community, one can say to him: Turn behind you

Rav Yehuda said Rav said: Why was Saul punished Because forwent his honor, as it is stated “And some base fellows said: How can this man save us? So they disparaged him and brought him no present. But he made himself as if he did not hear” “And Nahash the Ammonite marched up and encamped against Jabesh-gilead”
And Rabbi Yoḥanan said in the name of Rabbi Shimon ben Yehotzadak: Any Torah scholar
